purpose: This repository handles data ingestion from various sources to a lending/selling database system. It primarily processes loan servicing data, SOFR rates, and contract information from different loan providers, and also collects metrics for monitoring the lending/selling platform's operational status.
businessFeatures: Loan servicing data ingestion from MPLs (Marketplace Lenders)
SOFR (Secured Overnight Financing Rate) data ingestion from Federal Reserve API
Contract data bootstrapping from external systems
Synchronization of loan statuses between Arix and Selling databases
CloudWatch metrics collection and reporting for operational dashboards
Servicing data processing from S3 CSV files

apis.purpose: Fetch SOFR rates for loan calculations

dataEntities.name: Contract
dataEntities.description: Loan contract terms and associated metadata

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core loan entity with origination and sale status

dataEntities.name: Sofr
dataEntities.description: SOFR rate data used for loan calculations

dataEntities.name: ServicingTimestamp
dataEntities.description: Tracks data ingestion timestamps for each MPL

dataEntities.name: ServicingValue
dataEntities.description: Loan servicing data values including interest and payment information

messagingPatterns.name: CloudWatch Metrics
messagingPatterns.description: Publishes operational metrics for loan processing and system health

findings.title: Hardcoded Database Connection in README
findings.description: The README contains hardcoded database credentials (P4ssw0rd) for testing. Even if this is just for documentation, it sets a bad security precedent and could be mistaken for real credentials. Replace with placeholders or environment variable references.

findings.title: Insufficient Exception Handling in SOFR Ingestion
findings.description: The SOFR ingestion code doesn't properly handle API connectivity failures or response validation errors. Failed data fetches could cause inconsistent loan calculations. Implement comprehensive retry logic and fallback mechanisms.

findings.title: Unvalidated CSV File Processing
findings.description: S3Utils processes CSV files without proper validation, which could lead to injection attacks if maliciously crafted CSVs are uploaded. Implement strict schema validation before processing files.
